key_strengths: Word count output with compliance verification is a fundamental and correctly implemented feature
Content proportion guidance (Background 15% / Methods 25% / Results 40% / Conclusion 20%) is practically calibrated
Compression rules explicitly state what to preserve vs compress vs cut, with 'never cut' list
Abbreviation economy rule (≤3 abbreviations in 250-word abstract) prevents common abstract quality issue

recommendations.title: Conference format list based on training knowledge without recency disclaimer
recommendations.problem: The supported conference table includes specific word limits (ASGCT 250, ASCO 260, etc.) based on training knowledge that may change with updated submission guidelines.
recommendations.root_cause: No recency caveat applied to conference-specific format requirements.
recommendations.fix: Add a recency note to the conference format table: 'Word limits and formats verified as of training knowledge. Always check the current call for abstracts for the target conference before submission.'

recommendations.title: No guidance on handling abstract submission systems with structured fields
recommendations.problem: Many conference submission portals require separate field inputs for each section (Background, Methods, etc.) rather than a single text block. The skill delivers a formatted single text without guidance on this.
recommendations.root_cause: Output format focuses on text quality rather than submission portal usability.
recommendations.fix: Add a submission note: 'If the conference portal uses separate input fields per section, copy each section independently. The word count limit may apply per section or to the total — check the portal instructions.'
